fields.description:
## Why
The generic Pass-2 verify contract applies to EVERY finding. Two new binary sub-answers close whole FP/defect classes; one gloss fixes a false-premise gap. Because they touch the generic per-finding contract they are designed + calibrated together.

## What (concrete targets confirmed by review)
- The sub-answer vocabulary is GRADED_BINARY in src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py; validity() in review_kernel/decide.py iterates GRADED_BINARY. "Participate in validity" therefore means: ADD each new sub-answer to GRADED_BINARY (shared kernel), and its question text to the verifier prompts (plan_review_verifier.md + _agentic.md).
- Add committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim {yes|no|insufficient} (unifies G-4 exclusions + G-7 assertions/hedges): "Does a committed element (an AC / decomposed task / concrete edit / scope exclusion) depend on this claim being true, while the plan provides NEITHER a verification NOR a defined behavior if it is false?" Materiality via "committed element"; keys on absence-of-(verification-OR-fallback).
- Add respects_artifact_altitude {yes|no|insufficient} (FP-1): "Does the finding demand detail or presume a choice this artifact level legitimately defers to a child?" Note: this is a graded VALIDITY input (a low score DROPS the FP via existing Pass-3 arithmetic) -- NOT a veto.
- Gloss evidence_entails_finding (FP-2): a finding demanding what the plan already states is a false-premise / null-delta finding -> not entailed. No new key.
- "both variants" = the two verifier prompt files (single-turn + agentic), not the sub-answer's value count.
- na-default both new keys so old sidecars stay comparable.

## Provenance
Labels like G-8 / FP-3a / R-6 / WS-N are provenance tags from the DSO gap-report evaluation (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md) + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020) -- they are NOT codebase symbols and cannot be grepped. Concrete code targets are named inline and in this ticket's file_impact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim + respects_artifact_altitude added to GRADED_BINARY (review_kernel/verify.py) and both verifier prompts; na-defaulting. Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k verifier` green.
- [ ] evidence_entails_finding restatement gloss present in both verifier prompts. Proof: `grep -l "already states" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier*.md` returns both files.
- [ ] Positive rationale recorded for graded-field vs prompt-gloss choice (contrast with the FP-2 gloss approach).
- [ ] Calibration case: a well-formed `## Assumptions` block with a stated fallback scores low validity -> dropped. Proof: add a recall case to tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py asserting it drops; run `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k assumptions_block`.
- [ ] Generic pass stays generic: no criterion-specific branching in Pass 2/3.
